headline: Microsoft is selling off four Xbox studios as part of significant gaming cuts
summary: Microsoft announces 4,800 layoffs, with over 30% impacting its Xbox division. Four Xbox game studios are being spun off to operate independently. These significant cuts affect nearly every part of the gaming business.

headline: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
summary: Microsoft announces 4,800 new layoffs, affecting approximately 2.1% of its workforce. This follows earlier cuts a year ago. Most job losses are concentrated in commercial sales and the Xbox division.

headline: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
summary: Wealthy Americans are increasingly using AI as a primary teaching tool for their children. This trend diverges from general public distrust in AI. Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha are pioneering these AI-driven educational models.

headline: Mr. Lif’s Emergency Rations EP is post-9/11 hip-hop at its most daring
summary: Mr. Lif's 'Emergency Rations' EP is celebrated as daring post-9/11 hip-hop. The album cemented Lif's status as a 'conscious rapper' of the era. Definitive Jux was a seminal label for experimental, politically charged hip-hop.

headline: LEGO Announces E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial Figure That's Roughly Quarter Life Size
summary: LEGO unveils a new E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial figure, roughly quarter life-size. The buildable model features movable appendages. It includes a light-up chest to simulate E.T.'s glowing heart.

headline: Steam Rules Must Change, Developer Says, After Players Praise Bitesize Game But Are Allowed to Refund It Anyway
summary: A game developer calls for changes to Steam's refund policy. Players are receiving refunds for a short game despite giving it positive praise. The current policy allows returns even after significant playtime and enjoyment.

headline: These Are the Top 5 Best Selling Games on Amazon So Far This Year
summary: Amazon's 2026 best-seller charts highlight the top 5 games. Nintendo titles continue to dominate alongside GTA 6. The list offers insights into the ongoing physical versus digital gaming debate.

headline: The Pokémon Company Just Matched Each of Japan's Top Professional Soccer Teams to a Pokémon
summary: The Pokémon Company has paired each of Japan's 60 J League soccer teams with a unique Pokémon. This initiative is part of Pokémon's 30th-anniversary celebrations. Each professional team now has its own designated Pokémon partner.

headline: Uefa says 'integrity of game at stake' after Fifa's decision to overturn US star's red card ban
summary: Uefa criticizes FIFA's decision to overturn Folarin Balogun's automatic red card ban. The reversal followed an intervention by US President Donald Trump. Uefa states the 'integrity of the game' is now at stake due to the decision.

headline: Buckingham Palace says Prince Harry will not stay there despite his team announcing he would
summary: Buckingham Palace clarifies Prince Harry will not be staying at the Palace. This contradicts earlier announcements made by his team. Royal sources state Harry missed the deadline to respond to accommodation offers.

headline: Amber heat-health alerts issued as UK could see 10 consecutive days of temperatures over 30C
summary: Amber heat-health alerts have been issued across the UK. Temperatures are predicted to exceed 30C for 10 consecutive days. This marks the third significant heatwave of the summer season.

headline: Pizza Express held inquiry into Andrew's Woking claim
summary: Pizza Express conducted an internal inquiry regarding Prince Andrew's alibi. The investigation focused on his claim of visiting their Woking branch. BBC Newsnight revealed details about the restaurant's internal probe.
